name: Skylos Verify Benchmark v0.1
description: Neutral-label benchmark for black-box evaluation of AI-code verification tools.

methodology_sources.name: OWASP Benchmark
methodology_sources.principle: Per-case expected true/false labels and scoreable tool output.

methodology_sources.name: NIST SAMATE/SARD
methodology_sources.principle: Known-bad and known-good artifacts with documented weaknesses.

methodology_sources.name: SWE-bench
methodology_sources.principle: Realistic software-engineering task framing from repository context.

cases.title: Stale helper name after an auth refactor
cases.scenario: A generated request handler calls an auth helper that sounds plausible but was never implemented after a refactor.

origin.basis: Common agent edit pattern: renamed helper in one module, stale reference in another.

cases.title: Invented keyword arguments on a strict installed API
cases.scenario: Generated code uses real requests APIs with invented retry/body keyword arguments.

origin.basis: Known SDK drift pattern: valid package and callable, invalid call signature.

cases.title: Invented method on an installed SDK client
cases.scenario: Generated code calls a plausible OpenAI SDK convenience method that is not part of the installed SDK surface.

origin.basis: Common agent failure: method is plausible from product concepts but absent in the installed package.

cases.title: Python dependency version does not exist
cases.scenario: Generated requirements pin a real package to a version that does not exist.

origin.basis: Dependency hallucination pattern: package exists, version is invented.

cases.title: Nested npm workspace pins a real package to an invented version
cases.scenario: A nested workspace manifest pins lodash to a version that should not exist.

origin.basis: Monorepo package edit with a plausible but invalid version.

cases.title: Compound partial edit with stale symbol, unfinished function, and SDK drift
cases.scenario: A generated billing edit mixes a stale local function name, an unfinished stub, and an invalid requests keyword.

origin.basis: Common multi-turn agent failure: several plausible edits are half-applied.

cases.title: Selected range is clean although later generated code is broken
cases.scenario: The file contains a later phantom call, but the selected range covers only a clean helper.

cases.title: File scope should suppress unrelated module defects
cases.scenario: A project has defects in two modules, but verification is scoped to the edited API module.

cases.title: Clean generated requests service
cases.scenario: Generated service code uses local helpers and requests with accepted arguments.

origin.basis: False-positive guard for common requests usage.

cases.title: Clean nested npm workspace
cases.scenario: Nested workspace manifest uses a known-good lodash version.

origin.basis: False-positive guard for dependency version checks.
